summary: The CI job failed because a `docker buildx bake` step failed when a Dockerfile RUN command tried to download a nydus binary from GitHub releases via `wget` and received exit code 1. The wget command could not successfully fetch the nydus release tarball, causing the build to fail.
root_cause: The `docker buildx bake` integration test failed because a Dockerfile RUN step executing `wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz` exited with code 1, indicating the download failed (likely a network error or unavailable release artifact).

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from the bake step showing the wget command failed with exit code 1

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: GitHub Actions error annotation confirming the bake action failed due to the wget command

evidence.quote: level=error msg="/moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve returned error: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = process \"/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz\" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1"
evidence.reason: BuildKit server-side error log confirming the RPC solve operation failed due to the wget exit code 1

suggested_fix: Investigate why the nydus release download is failing - check if the $NYDUS_VERSION variable resolves to a valid release tag on GitHub, verify network connectivity to github.com from the CI runner, or pin to a known-good release version. Also consider adding retry logic or a fallback mirror for the download.

summary: The test `tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with a transient network error.
root_cause: GitHub returned too many 502 errors when pip tried to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`, causing the test to fail with `ResponseError('too many 502 error responses')`.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from pip showing that the network request to GitHub failed with repeated 502 responses, a transient server-side error.
The test was retried 3 times (via pytest-rerunfailures or similar), confirming the flaky/transient nature of the failure.
This is the final test failure summary confirming which test failed and why (pip returned exit code 1 due to the network error).

suggested_fix: This is a transient network failure caused by GitHub returning 502 errors. Re-running the CI job should resolve it. Alternatively, consider hosting the test package on a more reliable server or mocking the network call in the test.

summary: A Jest test in `test/config/get.ts` failed because `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returned a URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) while the test expected no trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). This is a behavior regression in the `pnpm config get` command introduced by the branch under test (`fix/11492`).
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command now appends a trailing slash to scoped registry URLs. The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` asserts the value equals `"https://my-org.registry.example.com"` but received `"https://my-org.registry.example.com/"`.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the failing test suite and test name.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: the command now returns a trailing slash that the test does not expect.
The test assertion at line 32 of test/config/get.ts that fails.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test suite and one test failed overall.

suggested_fix: In the `pnpm config get` implementation, strip the trailing slash from registry URLs before outputting them (or normalize the URL), so that scoped registry values like `@my-org:registry` are returned without a trailing slash, matching the expected format.
